_description: Maps Recovery task types and workflow stages to specific ACT-I beings. Each being has a domain of expertise, and tasks are routed to the being best suited for the current stage of work. The 'owner_agent_id' field in the tasks table gets set to the being's runtime identifier.

beings._description: Recovery's specialist beings and their domains. These map to real owner_agent_id values in the Bomba SR tasks table.

agreement-maker.domain: Closing deals, negotiating terms, formalizing partnerships, contract management
agreement-maker.capacity_note: Highest impact per task — focus on fewer, higher-value items

connector.domain: Building relationships, networking, introductions, outreach, rapport building
connector.capacity_note: High-volume relationship touchpoints — can handle more tasks simultaneously

keeper.domain: Account retention, health monitoring, churn prevention, renewal management, client success
keeper.capacity_note: Proactive monitoring role — handles many accounts with alert-driven intervention

multiplier.domain: Amplification, PR execution, thought leadership, content distribution, brand building
multiplier.capacity_note: Campaign-oriented — tasks tend to be longer-running with multiple deliverables

routing_rules._description: Primary routing table. For each task_type + stage combination, specifies which being should own the task. This is the source of truth for auto_routing in automation_triggers.json.

connector_to_agreement-maker.reason: Relationship is established, now needs negotiation expertise
connector_to_agreement-maker.handoff_protocol: Connector provides relationship context, communication style notes, and key stakeholder map to agreement-maker
Connector documents mutual value prop, partner expectations, and relationship dynamics

agreement-maker_to_multiplier.reason: Terms set, needs go-to-market execution and amplification
agreement-maker_to_multiplier.handoff_protocol: Agreement-maker provides partnership terms, deliverables, and co-marketing permissions

multiplier_to_keeper.reason: Launch complete, needs ongoing health management
multiplier_to_keeper.handoff_protocol: Multiplier provides activation results, contact cadence, and success metrics baseline

retention._notes: Retention is keeper-dominated. The keeper owns the full lifecycle. Only exception: if intervention requires renegotiating terms, agreement-maker may be pulled in as a consultant (not a routing change).

multiplier_to_connector.reason: Content is ready, now needs relationship-based media pitching
multiplier_to_connector.handoff_protocol: Multiplier provides press kit, key messages, approved pitches, and target outlet list

connector_to_multiplier.reason: Pitches sent, now needs monitoring and amplification of results
connector_to_multiplier.handoff_protocol: Connector provides pitch responses, committed coverage, and follow-up schedule

fallback_routing._description: When the designated being is unavailable or overloaded, tasks fall back in this priority order.

load_balancing._description: Simple capacity-based routing. If a being exceeds max_active_tasks, new tasks route to fallback.

_rationale.agreement-maker: Complex negotiations need focus — fewer tasks, deeper engagement
_rationale.connector: Relationship touchpoints can be higher volume — more tasks, lighter per-task effort
_rationale.keeper: Monitoring many accounts with alert-driven intervention — highest capacity
_rationale.multiplier: Campaigns need sustained effort — moderate capacity
